free_text: Prompt gamma rays induced by inelastic scattering of
FRM II research reactor at Forschungs-
Prompt gamma-ray analysis. The cross sections in "mb"
represent the gamma-rays emission probabilities at an
angle of 90 degrees between neutron beam and detector
including the solid angle supported by the detector.
Iron foil with mass of 1.282 g

The thermal neutrons were filtered from the
fast neutron beam by boron carbide plate with a
thickness of 1 cm and two lead plates with a total
thickness of 5 cm. The fast neutron spectrum in the
energy range 0.075-20 MeV at the sample position was
determined by means of the foil activation technique.
Col. 1: Incident neutron energy min. value (MeV)
Col. 2: Incident neutron energy max. value (MeV)
